scenic.text: Uma walked us through the Cornwall coastline and the Major-General’s estate — could we get the ground plan to SMs by Wednesday?

costumes.text: Theo measurements scheduled for Saturday — cast received the link.

sound.text: Discussed mic plot: 14 channels expected. Wes confirming with the rental house.

music.text: Marcus shared the vocal score; piano-vocal parts going out by EOD tomorrow.

production.text: Welcome to the company! Lovely first day, voices already sound strong.

timeBlocks.activity: Music rehearsal — "Pour, O pour the pirate sherry" + "Oh, better far to live and die"
Music rehearsal — "When Frederic was a little lad" (Ruth's ballad) + ensemble cleanup

costumes.text: Pirate King’s coat — Cassidy needs the back gusset wider for the chorus choreo. Theo watching on Friday.

sound.text: Could we get Mabel’s mic level discussion on the agenda for tomorrow’s production meeting? Brilliance is sitting under the brass at the top of Act 1.

music.text: Pirate King’s patter pickups in "Oh, better far" landing cleanly — great work Cassidy.
Ruth’s tempo in "When Frederic was a little lad" still settling; Marcus and Elena working it 1-on-1 Saturday.

production.text: Elena 12 minutes late — academic conflict, confirmed for next call.

timeBlocks.activity: Stage Act I, "I am the very model of a modern Major-General" + "Climbing over rocky mountain"
Stage Act II, "When the foeman bares his steel" + Sergeant's "When a felon's not engaged in his employment"

scenic.text: The downstage left rock unit is in the dancers’ main entrance lane — could we shift it 2’3′ upstage? Riley walking through tomorrow.

costumes.text: Major-General’s helmet plume keeps catching on the proscenium during his entrance — would a softer plume work, or trim 1–2 inches?
Police helmets fitted for the ensemble. Felix's strap snapped — we have a spare; replacement ordered.

wigsMakeup.text: Ruth’s grey wig look approved by director; Sofia building the final.

props.text: Cutlasses (×7) need to be returned to rehearsal stock after the prop shop’s safety check tomorrow.
Police whistles — currently using rehearsal whistles. Final batch arriving Monday.

lighting.text: Vivian present — wanted to flag that the "creep" cue in Act II is loving the bumps in tempo we built in. Looks great.

sound.text: Quinn confirmed mic 4 cable is the culprit on the intermittent crackle — swapped during break, no further issues tonight.

music.text: "Climbing over rocky mountain" tempo at ♪=132 felt right. Locking in for tomorrow’s sitzprobe.
Major-General patter — cleaner than yesterday. Devon is finding the breath.

production.text: 12-min hold during the police entrance staging — sorting traffic between the cutlass exit and the police entrance. Resolved by re-ordering exits.

lineNotes.scriptedText: Oh, false one, you have deceiv’d me!
lineNotes.comment: Dropped after Pirate King's exit — picked up on the next page.

lineNotes.scriptedText: Yes, I am brave! Oh, family descent,
lineNotes.spokenText: Yes — I am brave. Oh, family descent,

lineNotes.scriptedText: I am the very model of a modern Major-General
lineNotes.spokenText: I am the very, very model of a modern Major-General
lineNotes.comment: Added "very" twice on the first iteration; second pass was clean.

lineNotes.scriptedText: When Frederic was a little lad he proved so brave and daring,
lineNotes.spokenText: When Frederic was a young lad he proved so brave and daring,

lineNotes.scriptedText: "When a felon's not engaged in his employment…"
lineNotes.comment: Sergeant came in about 2 beats late — cue light was on, internal cue clear at tomorrow’s music call.

props.name: Cutlasses (×7)
props.notes: Soft-tipped — fight choreography approved by combat coach; daily safety check before each call.

props.name: Major-General’s helmet (plumed)
props.notes: Plume length being trimmed by Theo — was catching on the proscenium.

props.name: Police whistles (×6)
props.notes: Final batch arriving 2026-07-12. Rehearsal whistles in use until then.

props.name: Pirate sherry bottle + cups (×6)
props.notes: Bottle filled with tea-water; cups never get a hard set-down (sound risk on mic).

props.name: Mabel’s parasol
props.notes: Period-style; mechanism delicate — open/close practice ongoing.

props.name: Major-General’s sword
props.notes: Ceremonial — stays sheathed throughout; safety-checked weekly.

props.name: Pirate flag (Jolly Roger)
props.notes: Hand-carried on entrances; tracking ID assigned to Kai (dance captain).

notes.text: Welcome to the company! Bring a pencil, a water bottle, and your vocal score.
Marcus will collect any vocal-range questions during the welcome.
Music rehearsal — pirates and principals. Off-book NOT required; off-score IS appreciated where possible.

scheduleItems.activity: "Pour, O pour the pirate sherry" + "Oh, better far"

notes.text: SITZPROBE — first time with the pit. No staging; this is purely vocal-with-orchestra. Come in performance voice (warmed up).
Mic packs distributed at the half by Quinn — see her at SR.
Costumes NOT required; wear black or neutrals.

scheduleItems.description: - Cast in order of script appearance

scheduleItems.activity: Marcus warm-up + balance check with pit

scheduleItems.description: - Top of show through Major-General’s entrance

notes.text: Designer run — designers and full production team in the house. Please be in full costume calls at the half.
Photo call directly after — plan to be in costume for ~30 minutes after final note.

scheduleItems.description: - Director’s photo list; ~30 min in costume

tracking.sceneLabel: ACT I — A rugged seashore on the coast of Cornwall

tracking.notes: Confirm sherry bottle + cups, cutlasses, flag on SR table
Pirates discovered — Pirate King center, Samuel SL, ensemble flanking
Frederic enters — his last day as a pirate apprentice
Ruth (the maid-of-all-work) enters
Pirates exit — leave Frederic to his fate
Ruth exits dejected after Frederic dismisses her
Major-General’s daughters — Mabel last, with parasol
Major-General Stanley’s grand entrance — plumed helmet, sword
LX cue 28 — sting at "I am the very model"
Pirates re-enter — they've come for the daughters
Major-General exits in despair (he has feigned being an orphan)
Act I finale — pirates and daughters; everyone clears for intermission
Strike sherry table SR; preset MG’s estate furniture
Battens 4 and 7 out; estate backdrop in

tracking.sceneLabel: ACT II — A ruined chapel by moonlight on the Major-General’s estate

tracking.notes: Major-General alone, troubled by his lie
Daughters enter to comfort him
Frederic enters — the moment of truth
Sergeant of Police + Police Ensemble, tiptoeing
Special on Sergeant for "When a felon's not engaged in his employment"
Daughters exit — the police are alone
Pirates re-enter — stealth approach, Ruth with them
Cutlasses out — pre-checked at the half
Major-General returns — caught between pirates and police
Daughters return — the standoff
Full company bow — Pirate King + Mabel + Frederic last
Principals’ final bow

blocking.notes: Daughters fan across, Mabel arrives last
Spotlight cue — "I am the very model"
Tiptoe entrance; comedic timing on sergeant’s whistle
Choreographed fight — weapons safety check before each call

holds.reason: Pit musician swapped a broken reed; held to keep balance with orchestra.

incidents.description: Mic 4 (Mabel) cut out briefly during "Poor Wandering One" — quick swap at the next exit; ~12 seconds of dropped audio in the verse.

costumes.text: Major-General’s plume now riding clean of the proscenium — thank you Theo.

lighting.text: LX 86 (Sergeant’s special) felt a touch hot — could we drop intensity 5%?

sound.text: Mic 4 dropout investigating tonight — Quinn replacing the cable before tomorrow’s show.

music.text: Marcus and the pit absolutely killed "Modern Major-General." Audience erupted.

production.text: House staff handled the 6 late-seaters cleanly during the overture vamp.

general.text: Magical opening. Standing ovation. Pit sounded gorgeous.

stageManagement.text: Strike: all cutlasses returned to locked cabinet, mic packs to dryer, parasol to SL table.

understudyChanges.reason: Greta out with strep — first u/s performance for Kai.

lighting.text: LX 86 trim looks great — perfect intensity now.

sound.text: Mic 4 cable replaced — no dropouts tonight. Thanks Quinn.

music.text: Pit was tight. Tempo on "Climbing over rocky mountain" landed at ♪=132 — chef’s kiss.

production.text: Kai was excellent on for Samuel. Confidence + clarity, blocking clean.

general.text: Smooth, tight run. Cleanest show so far — 7 minutes shorter than opening, which feels right (no holds).

scenes.numberName: When Frederic Was a Little Lad

scenes.numberName: When the Foeman Bares His Steel
